title: Compare Fractions with Same Denominator
description: Determines which fraction is greater when denominators are equal by comparing numerators.
condition: Two fractions with the same denominator (e.g., a/b and c/b).
response: The fraction with the larger numerator is greater.
label: Principle / Model
example: Compare $\frac{3}{8}$ and $\frac{4}{8}$. Since $4 > 3$, $\frac{4}{8} > \frac{3}{8}$.

title: Compare Fractions with Same Numerator
description: When two fractions have the same numerator, the fraction with the smaller denominator is greater because its parts are larger.
condition: Two fractions with the same numerator (e.g., a/b and a/c).
response: Compare denominators; the fraction with the smaller denominator is greater.
label: Principle / Model
example: Compare $\frac{1}{3}$ and $\frac{1}{2}$. Since thirds are smaller pieces than halves, $\frac{1}{3} < \frac{1}{2}$.
notes: Requires understanding that more equal parts means smaller pieces.

title: Connect Fraction to Shaded Parts
description: Interpret a fraction $\frac{a}{b}$ as $a$ shaded parts out of $b$ equal parts of a whole.
condition: Given a fraction $\frac{a}{b}$ and a diagram partitioned into equal parts.
response: Identify or create $b$ equal parts and shade or verify that $a$ of those parts are shaded.
label: Rule / Plan
example: For $\frac{3}{4}$, divide the whole into 4 equal parts and shade 3 of them.
notes: Core interpretation of fraction notation as parts of a whole.

title: Count Equal Parts Between Whole Numbers
description: Determine the denominator by counting the number of equal intervals between two consecutive whole numbers on a number line.
condition: A number line segment between two consecutive whole numbers (e.g., 0 and 1) is partitioned into equal intervals.
response: Count the number of equal intervals to determine the denominator (number of equal parts per whole).
label: Principle / Model
example: If the segment from 0 to 1 is divided into 6 equal parts, then each part represents $\frac{1}{6}$ and the denominator is 6.

title: Count Shaded Equal Parts
description: Identifies the fraction represented by counting shaded parts out of equal total parts.
condition: A whole partitioned into equal parts with some parts shaded.
response: Write fraction as (number of shaded parts)/(total equal parts).
label: Principle / Model
example: If 5 of 8 equal parts are shaded, the fraction is $\frac{5}{8}$.

title: Extend Unit Fraction Partition Beyond 1
description: Continue counting equal unit fractions past 1 to locate fractions greater than one.
condition: A number line is partitioned into equal parts per whole and a fraction greater than 1 (e.g., $\frac{5}{4}$) must be located.
response: Count unit fractions past 1 using the same interval size to locate the improper fraction.
label: Principle / Model
example: If each interval is $\frac{1}{4}$, then $\frac{5}{4}$ is one interval past 1 because $\frac{4}{4}=1$ and $\frac{5}{4}=1+\frac{1}{4}$.

title: Generate Equivalent Fraction by Scaling
description: Creates an equivalent fraction by multiplying numerator and denominator by the same nonzero number.
condition: A fraction a/b and a chosen whole number k.
response: Compute (a×k)/(b×k) to form an equivalent fraction.
label: Principle / Model

title: Identify Equal Partitions
description: Recognize whether a whole is partitioned into equal-sized parts.
condition: Given a diagram partitioned into multiple parts.
response: Determine whether all parts are equal in size.
label: Concept
example: A rectangle split into 6 congruent sections shows equal partitions; unequal sections do not represent sixths.
notes: Students may count parts without checking for equal size.

title: Identify Equivalent Fractions
description: Recognizes two fractions as equivalent when they represent the same quantity.
condition: Two fractions a/b and c/d.
response: Determine they are equivalent if they represent the same point on a number line or same portion of a whole.
label: Principle / Model
example: $\frac{2}{6}$ and $\frac{1}{3}$ are equivalent because both represent one-third of the whole.

title: Interpret Denominator as Number of Equal Parts
description: Understands that the denominator tells how many equal parts make up one whole.
condition: A fraction written as a/b.
response: State that b represents the number of equal parts in one whole.
label: Concept
example: In $\frac{5}{6}$, the 6 means the whole is divided into 6 equal parts.

title: Interpret Numerator as Selected Parts
description: Understands that the numerator tells how many equal parts are being considered.
condition: A fraction written as a/b.
response: State that a represents the number of equal parts counted or shaded.
label: Concept
example: In $\frac{5}{6}$, the 5 means 5 of the 6 equal parts are selected.

title: Locate Fraction by Counting Unit Fractions
description: Identify the position of a fraction on a number line by counting forward a given number of equal unit intervals from 0 or another whole number.
condition: A number line is partitioned into equal intervals and a fraction $\frac{a}{b}$ with known unit size $\frac{1}{b}$ must be located.
response: Count forward $a$ intervals of size $\frac{1}{b}$ from 0 (or the nearest whole) and mark that position.
label: Principle / Model
example: If each interval is $\frac{1}{6}$, then the first tick after 0 represents $\frac{1}{6}$. If each interval is $\frac{1}{4}$, the third tick after 0 represents $\frac{3}{4}$.

title: Locate Fraction on Number Line
description: Places a fraction on a number line by partitioning the interval from 0 to 1 into equal parts.
condition: Given a fraction a/b and a number line from 0 to 1.
response: Partition the interval into b equal parts and mark the ath tick from 0.
label: Principle / Model
example: To plot $\frac{1}{4}$, divide 0–1 into 4 equal parts and mark the first tick.

title: Partition Shape into Equal Parts
description: Divide a given whole into a specified number of equal parts.
condition: Given a whole shape and a specified number $b$ of parts.
response: Create $b$ equal-sized partitions of the whole.
label: Production / Skill
example: To represent $\frac{5}{6}$, divide a rectangle into 6 equal sections.
notes: Procedural drawing skill; equality of parts is essential.

title: Recognize Non-Equivalent Fractions
description: Determine that two fractions are not equal when they do not represent the same quantity.
condition: Given two fractions a/b and c/d where a×d ≠ b×c.
response: State that the fractions are not equivalent.
label: Concept
example: $\frac{1}{2}$ and $\frac{2}{3}$ are not equivalent because $1\times3 \neq 2\times2$.
notes: Response is always the same conclusion (not equivalent) when condition is met.

title: Recognize Unit Fraction from Partition
description: Identify that each equal part between whole numbers represents the unit fraction with denominator equal to the number of parts.
condition: A whole is divided into $b$ equal parts on a number line.
response: State that each part represents $\frac{1}{b}$.
label: Principle / Model
example: If 1 whole is divided into 4 equal parts, each part is $\frac{1}{4}$.

title: Represent Whole Number as Fraction
description: Writes a whole number as a fraction with a chosen denominator.
condition: A whole number n and a chosen denominator b.
response: Write n as (n×b)/b.
label: Principle / Model
example: Write 2 in fourths: $2 = \frac{8}{4}$.

title: Select Appropriate Comparison Symbol
description: Choose the correct symbol (<, >, =) to represent the relationship between two quantities.
condition: Two quantities have been compared and their relative sizes determined.
response: Write < if left is less than right, > if greater, or = if equal.
label: Production / Skill
example: Since $\frac{3}{2} > \frac{3}{4}$, write $\frac{3}{2} > \frac{3}{4}$.

title: Select Diagram Matching a Given Fraction
description: Evaluate multiple diagrams and select those that correctly represent a specified fraction of a whole.
condition: Given several diagrams and a target fraction $\frac{a}{b}$.
response: Choose all diagrams where exactly $a$ of $b$ equal parts are shaded.
label: Rule / Plan
example: Select diagrams where 3 of 4 equal parts are shaded to match $\frac{3}{4}$.
notes: Requires coordinating equal partitions with shaded count.

title: Select True Fraction Equations
description: Evaluate multiple fraction equations and select those that are true based on equivalence.
condition: Given a list of fraction equalities.
response: Identify and select the equations where both fractions are equivalent.
label: Rule / Plan
example: From the list, select $\frac{2}{6}=\frac{1}{3}$ and $\frac{4}{8}=\frac{3}{6}$ because each pair simplifies to the same value.
notes: Requires repeated application of fraction equivalence.

title: Use Known Fraction to Locate 1
description: Determine the location of 1 on a number line by reasoning about how many unit fractions compose one whole.
condition: A fraction such as $\frac{a}{b}$ is labeled on a number line and the unit fraction $\frac{1}{b}$ is implied by equal partitions.
response: Count or reason that $b$ unit fractions $\frac{1}{b}$ equal 1, and locate 1 accordingly.
label: Principle / Model
example: If $\frac{3}{4}$ is marked and each part is $\frac{1}{4}$, then $\frac{4}{4}=1$, so 1 is one more $\frac{1}{4}$ past $\frac{3}{4}$.
